## [00:00 - 03:06]

I got a question recently on Facebook and this particular person when they asked me the question I clicked on their profile to see where they're from and I noticed they're from Scotland so the question they asked me was Hello Vin, I'm just wondering if somebody's just starting up their journey on improving their communication skills what top three things would you get them to focus on? Thank you for this question my Scottish friend and in this video I'm going to get into the top three things you can do to help you improve your communication skills let's get into it now. The first thing you can do and the first thing you must do is remove auditory clutter what I mean by auditory clutter is remove your non words and your filler words non words being the sounds that you make to fill the silence those things filler words being the words that you use to fill the silence and so like do you know what I mean when you use lots of non words and filler words meaning there's more verbal clutter in the way that you communicate the following occurs the first thing is that it decreases the clarity of your message the second thing it's going to rob you of credibility the third thing it's going to rob you of authority and the fourth thing is that it makes you seem as if you're unsure about what you're talking about even if you are sure about what you're talking about it seems like you're not. I'd love to throw you to an example of one of my students I want to show you them before when there's lots of filler words and non words and then I want to show you what happens when we remove the filler words and non words from their communication watch how powerful this example is let me let me grab the video from the top and pull it down just because I wanted to challenge myself I signed up for a 300 level history course American Revolution and as we were kind of winding through our class we'd kind of gotten through most of the inciting incidents to the American Revolution and and the professor he would always sit at the edge of a table with his notes right beside him and so you know me the youngest student in the class by about a year and a half and he looked out at the class and said now why do you guys think it was important that the that the signers of the declaration addressed it to to the king so yeah reason I'm telling you all this is that wound up being four years later became jack of all trades master of two yeah you got this you got us sit at the edge of the table notes next to him and he posed the question of the class why was it important that the framers of the Declaration of Independence

why was it important for them to address the declaration to the king and not to Parliament and timidly raised my hand and said because it was a revocation of everything it was to be British he paused too much and then it distracts people from the message and then causes people to have less respect for you because you can't dance and you've discovered my kryptonite I can't dance and I got to tell people that I just met for the first time being an extra and when I was dancing I literally I literally danced like this and I did this for 30 minutes in front of strangers as my face was going be true red back to what I was saying when it comes to visual clutter that's the visual tics that you may have body language tics let me show with you a couple of mine the first one was the classic tilting of the glasses when I first started my speaking career I would know where to go I do this at least 50 to 100 times when I'm on stage because this was my nervous tick when I get nervous I keep adjusting my glasses and the pace at which I adjust my glasses will increase as I become more nervous it was the most mental thing and it drove people insane and the second thing that I would do which was again it's a form of visual clutter was I'd use the same hand gestures and I wouldn't have any variety in my hand gestures and when I speak I just I just keep doing different pieces of this so I'd go really fast if I'm excited and then I'd slow down as I become less excited it was it was ridiculous for clarity's sake visual clutter is when you're using your body language in a non-functional way where it doesn't support the things you're saying let's go to a student example in this video the visual clutter takes the form of movement and I want to show you how when my students move around with no real purpose how it can be a form of visual clutter and distract people from the message and watch the before and after I think you'll find this one also very inspiring and inspiring because the smallest changes can have the most drastic impact on the way people perceive you and your message so as we throw to this video

## [09:15 - 10:47]

you do that you're doing a Dishin smile and a Dishin smile is when you're smiling your teeth are showing your cheeks are moved up and you've got the crow's feet appearing around your eyes I am guilty of not doing this smile enough I'm going to show you a few photos where I do the classic photo pose smile have a look at that cheesy smile not even real look at vin been faking phonia not sincere or authentic that's me that's what I used to do but the moment I learned this let me show you an example of me doing a real smile look that just looks way more authentic and way real it looks like I'm genuinely happy as opposed to posing for a bloody photo these are the three things that I would encourage all of my students do especially those who are early on and just starting their communication skills journey make sure you remove the auditory clutter make sure you remove the visual clutter and smile more it may seem super simple but these three things can completely change the way other people perceive you thanks for joining me for this week's video everyone if you've got any questions on communication skills or anything you'd like to ask me please do leave them in the comment section below I love getting questions because you can see things I can't there are times you're going to come up with a topic that I wouldn't even thought of so I'd love to hear from you drop me a comment down below see you next week
